title: Sibbaldia perpusilla
intro: Sibbaldia perpusilla, also known as Tibetan rose or alpine golden-rod, is a flowering plant species native to the Himalayan region. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Sibbaldia perpusilla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Tibetan medicine, Sibbaldia perpusilla is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Sibbaldia perpusilla is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It belongs to the genus Sibbaldia, which is a group of plants that are native to the mountainous regions of Asia. The family Rosaceae is a large and diverse group of plants that includes many common species such as apples and roses.
